data.narrative:
Quandela is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Valerian Giesz, Pascale Senellart, and Niccolo Somaschi. The quantum computing sector comprises a modest universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and Rigetti Computing.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing subsector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—only 1 Series B company exists within the narrow peer set, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum required for reliable median triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given the heterogeneity of quantum approaches and commercial maturity across the universe.

Without a sufficiently dense peer cluster or disclosed financials, this analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range. Resolution requires either: (1) disclosure of Quandela's revenue or burn metrics to enable earlier-stage SaaS or hardware benchmarks; (2) subsequent funding events among the identified comparables to establish Series B pricing; or (3) expansion of the dataset to include non-quantum hardware or deep-tech Series B companies with comparable development timelines. The key uncertainty is whether Quandela's photonic approach commands a valuation premium or discount relative to superconducting and trapped-ion peers.
